mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal fails to predict direction in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting higher corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or flat 60-day forward return in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive short-term market sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are uncorrelated with INDPRO changes during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive monetary policy and elevated unemployment creates a stagflationary risk environment that suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns, with SPX posting positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth reflects expanding economic activity and corporate profit margins, which directly supports higher equity prices and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently fail to predict 20-day forward returns, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.05 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the inflation-to-return transmission breaks down when growth remains strong.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual labor signal produces flat or negative 5-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction term shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.
